author: mysticryuujin
content:
Chase plans to do nothing, Chase has brought up OOMs in debugs for years lol

Erigon is or was capable of streaming, Nethermind is also capable of streaming, beyond that I can't make 600GB smaller lol

author: mysticryuujin
content:
Here's a 5 year old geth issue

https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/issues/22244

author: ahamlat
content: If erigon supports streaming, there shouldn't be an OOM error on this block

author: mysticryuujin
content:
I can check on that, but 99.999% of RPC endpoints are not direct connections to nodes, they're some kinda proxy that may or may not also stream.

Erigon used to stream, I know that for sure, I assume it still can if it's direct.

author: ahamlat
content:
What I'm trying to understand is how big can be an output from debug_traceBlock, and I got lucky with that block as it is really big. With block gas limit increase, this will get even bigger, there some interesting suggestions to reduce the trace size : https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/762#discussion_r3043703653
https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/762#discussion_r3059867903

author: mysticryuujin
content:
Erigon may have disabled streaming by default, cuz they used to break and return invalid JSON when there was a panic/crash/whatever

We have acknowledged that the method isn't perfect and there are issues, but these are issues that have existed for like 10 years at this point.

If we have to go completely back to the drawing board and re-architect the opcode tracer then once again perfection will be the enemy of progress, and for the 87th time the effort to standardization of the method will fail.

author: mysticryuujin
content:
The gas limit isn't even the problem, there are individual transactions, like the one linked in the geth issue that are also ridiculous

https://etherscan.io/tx/0xd80a10106f2a8fb76cf6db6b9f7a64f08257b4952c94c888e1ac41ceb80733c9

author: mysticryuujin
content: fwiw I just sat down to check, Erigon does stream `< Transfer-Encoding: chunked` and does not OOM and is documented via the flag `--rpc.streaming.disable`

author: mysticryuujin
content:
haha it didn't OOM but it did produce invalid JSON:
`jq: parse error: Unfinished JSON term at EOF at line 1, column -1578612014`

author: mysticryuujin
content: hmmm maybe it did OOM

author: mysticryuujin
content:
Nethermind also has an execution timeout, but if we specify timeout value in the request, we also hit their default 20s json-rpc timeout.

The QuickNode team will test again later today with a node with a longer timeout 😂
